sourceMovie.title: Hercules
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Hercules (1997) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Aladdin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Aladdin draws from the same animation well as Hercules, channeling family in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1992 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Super Mario Bros. Movie (2023) captures the animation appeal of Hercules without simply imitating it. The family streak running through both films is what puts The Super Mario Bros. Movie at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Croods: A New Age
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The animation appeal of Hercules — that funny edge, the family — translates cleanly into The Croods: A New Age (2020). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Moana 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Moana 2 (2024) clearly understood what makes animation work — the same quality that keeps Hercules rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Monkey King
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Monkey King (2023) is the kind of film Hercules fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The animation overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: My Little Pony: A New Generation
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: My Little Pony: A New Generation shares Hercules's gift for animation — the 2021 film delivers family without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Spellbound
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Hercules compelling — the animation, the family — shows up in Spellbound (2024) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Moana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The animation energy of Hercules is alive in Moana (2016), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The Boxtrolls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Boxtrolls (2014) operates in the same animation as Hercules, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Strange World
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Strange World (2022) belongs to the same conversation as Hercules — both anchor their animation in family emotion that doesn't let go. The uplifting undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hercules?
faq.answer: Try Aladdin, The Super Mario Bros. Movie, The Croods: A New Age—each shares DNA with Hercules while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hercules?
faq.answer: Hercules (1997) is best described as Animation, Family, Fantasy, Adventure, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hercules part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Hercules (Disney) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
